topic: Advanced catalogue strategy — what separates good from great
hook: Month 1 you learned what sync is.

slides.eyebrow: Education · Advanced catalogue strategy — what separates good from great
slides.headline:
Month 1 you learned what sync is.
Month 2 you learned what sync pays.
slides.lede: Month 3 question: what separates a catalogue that gets briefs from one that doesn't? ↓ · Here's what you need to know: — — — · DEPTH OVER WIDTH

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: Depth means: · Every track has a complete metadata record · Every track has stems prepared and labelled · Every co-writer credit is documented and signed
list_items: Every track has a complete metadata record
Every track has stems prepared and labelled
Every co-writer credit is documented and signed
Rights are confirmed on every piece before any brief arrives

slides.headline:
Width means: you have a lot of stuff.
Supervisors can't use "a lot of stuff."
slides.lede: Here's what you need to know: — — — · MOODBOARD YOUR CATALOGUE · Great catalogue managers don't think in track titles. They think in placement types.

slides.headline:
Build a mood index:
— Upbeat / celebratory (sports, celebration, product
slides.lede:
launch)
— Tension / build (thriller, drama, documentary)
— Intimate / emotive (healthcare, charity, human interest)
— Epic / cinematic (films, streaming originals, trailers)
— Background / atmospheric (hospitality, retail, ambient brand)

slides.headline: Every track should have a primary and secondary placement type.
slides.lede: Supervisors search this way. Speak their language. · Here's what you need to know: — — — · VERSIONING IS A COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE

slides.headline: Most composers submit one version.
slides.lede: The composers getting repeat briefs submit five. · Here's what you need to know: — — — · THE TECHNICAL FLOOR

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede:
No brief gets fulfilled without these: · WAV masters at 24-bit / 48kHz minimum · ISRC registered · Stems labelled by instrument group (not by track number) · This isn't advanced practice.
It's the baseline.
list_items: No brief gets fulfilled without these:
Stems labelled by instrument group (not by track number)

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
